summary: Soundboard capture of a solid ’89 show, with a standout Loser and a blazing China Cat Sunflower → The Rider transition.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard tape that many reviewers praise as “incredible” and well‑balanced. Musically the concert delivers several highlights – an “spectacular” Loser, a superb China Cat Sunflower into The Rider segue, plus strong performances of Good Times and Box of Rain – though a few note it isn’t a career‑defining night.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers are enthusiastic about both the performance (standout songs, strong transitions) and the recording quality; only one review calls it merely “good but not special,” yielding a slightly mixed overall sentiment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced soundboard tape with no reported dropouts or major issues; reviewers repeatedly commend the mix and mastering by Charlie Miller.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall solid flow with a particularly praised China>Rider transition; the show builds nicely from Good Times opening to a memorable Box of Rain encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is noted as a bit strained on this tour.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work on the encore (Box of Rain) is highlighted as a key moment that pushes the show over the edge.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland steps up nicely, delivering an impressive organ solo that fans loved.
